monday-to-frida: Monday to Friday, 8am to 4:30pm (closed on weekends and bank holidays).

fy-warning-text: For the 2025 to 2026 financial year, there will be a single payment covering quarters 1 and 2.

all-content-is-: All content is available under the Open Government Licence v3.0, except where otherwise stated

you-do-not: You do not have a service role
to-access-this: To access this content, your Defra account administrator must assign you a service role within the Local Authority Payments (LAPs) service.
extended-producer: Extended producer responsibility (EPR) for packaging for local authorities
when-and-how: when and how you will receive payments and payment information
you-can-use: You can use any of the following methods to contact us.

we-will-contact: We will contact you by email if we:

ask-question: ask a question about your payments
you-do: You do not have permission to access this page.
you-need-to: You need to sign in to view this page.
request-to-update-bank-details: CEOs and Heads of Finance can
submit-a-request: submit a request to update local authority’s bank details.
request-bank-details-update: Update your local authority bank details
request-to: Update your local authority bank details
we-need-your: We need your local authority's latest bank details to make sure pEPR payments are sent to the correct account.
submit-your: Submit your updated bank details securely using the online form
we-will: We will contact your local authority’s authorised officer to confirm the details
you-will-receive: You will receive an email once your updated bank details are verified and approved
must-be-between: Must be between 6 and 8 digits long
we-will-verify: We will verify the information with your local authority’s authorised officer.
you-will-receive-a-confirmation: You will receive a confirmation email once the update is approved.
accountName: Enter the name on the account
sortCodePattern: Enter a valid sort code like 309430
sortCodeLength: Sort code must be 6 digits long
accountNumberDigits: Enter a valid account number like 00733445
accountNumberRange: Account number must be between 6 and 8 digits
we-use-essential-cookies: We use some essential cookies to make this service work.
what-are-cookies: Cookies are small files saved on your phone, tablet or computer when you visit a website.
why-use-cookies: We use cookies to make this site work and collect information about how you use our service.
what-essential-cookies-do: Essential cookies keep your information secure while you use this service. We do not need to ask permission to use them.
name: Name
session-cookie-use: Used to identify and persist a unique session for each user/browser
user-session-cookie-use: Used to keep you signed in
cookie_preferences_set_use: Lets us know that you've saved your cookie consent settings
locale-cookie-use: Used to set your language preference
csrf-token: Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) token storage
no-payment-docs: You have no payment documents to view.
accessibility-statement-for-laps: Accessibility statement for the Local Authority Payments service (LAPs)
accessibility-statement-applies-to: This accessibility statement applies to the https://epr-local-authority-payments.defra.gov.uk/ website. It is limited to this specific subdomain and sitemap and does not cover other GOV.UK subdomains such as primary GOV.UK subdomains (for example www.gov.uk) or the blog subdomain (www.blog.gov.uk), which have their own accessibility statements.
website-run-by: This website is run by the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (Defra) and the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) scheme administrator PackUK.
how-to-use-the-site: How you should be able to use this website
many-people-can-use: We want as many people as possible to be able to use this website. You should be able to:
change-color: change colours, contrast levels and fonts using browser or device settings
zoom-in: zoom in up to 400% without the text spilling off the screen
navigate-with-keyboard: navigate most of the website using just a keyboard or speech recognition software
listen-to-website: listen to most of the website using a screen reader (including the most recent versions of JAWS, NVDA and VoiceOver)
simple-text: We also make the website text as simple as possible to understand.
advice-by-AbilityNet: AbilityNet has advice on making your device easier to use if you have a disability.
parts-of-site: Parts of this website are not fully accessible. For example:
docs-not-in-eng: some pages and documents are not written in plain English
decorative-images-no-in-eng: some decorative images have generic image descriptions
reduced-colour-contrast: some links and text have a reduced level of colour contrast
inconsistent-text-style: some text styling in tables is not consistent
link-purpose-unclear: some links do not have clearly described purpose
pdfs-not-fully-accessible: many documents are in PDF format and are not fully accessible
connected-services: some connected services (e.g. to enable login, helpdesk ticketing and support) do not currently meet accessibility standards. Please refer to their accessibility statements when using these services
service-in-welsh: some connected services are not translated into Welsh
tell-us: Tell us if you need information in a different format.
accessible-format-item-url: the web address (URL) of the content
accessible-format-item-format: the format you need - for example, plain text, braille, BSL, large print or audio CD
request-accessible-pdf: You can request a PDF in an accessible format by contacting the service’s helpdesk by email or by
raising-a-ticket: raising a ticket through the site
accessible-document-policy: You can also view Defra’s accessible document policy to report any problems or request documents in an alternative format.
reporting-accessibility-issues: Reporting accessibility problems with this website
any-problems-contact-us: If you find any problems that are not listed on this page or you think we’re not meeting the accessibility requirements, contact us.
equality-and-human-rights: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) is responsible for enforcing the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018 (the ‘accessibility regulations’).
no-happy-with-response: If you’re not happy with how we respond to your complaint, contact the Equality Advisory and Support Service (EASS).
technical-information-heading: Technical information about this website’s accessibility
technical-information-description: The Government Digital Service is committed to making its websites accessible, in accordance with the Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018.
compliance-status-description: This website is partially compliant with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines version 2.2 AA standard, due to the non-compliances and exemptions listed below.
non-compliance-abbreviations: Some page titles utilise abbreviations (e.g. EPR, LAPs) which are not defined or expanded upon. Users may be unfamiliar with the abbreviated terms. This fails WCAG 2.2 success criterion 2.4.2 Page Titled (Level A)
non-compliance-documents: Documents are in non-HTML formats, for example PDF. They should meet the standard for accessible PDFs including tagging and appropriate document structure
non-compliance-policy-link: View the Defra accessible document policy to report any problems or request documents in an alternative format.
out-of-scope: Content that’s not within the scope of the accessibility regulations
not-fix-pdfs: The accessibility regulations do not require us to fix PDFs or other documents published before 23 September 2018 if they’re not essential to providing our services.
improvements-heading: What we’re doing to improve accessibility
improvements-description: We are working alongside other government departments and agencies to fix content which fails to meet the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines version 2.2 AA standard.
prepared-date: This statement was prepared on 17 June 2026.
review-date: It was last reviewed on 17 June 2026.
testing: This website was last tested in June 2026 against the WCAG 2.2 AA standard. This test of a representative sample of pages was carried out by DAC and Accenture.
additional-testing: We also used findings from our own testing when preparing this accessibility statement.
